items.headline: New law strengthening water protection in EU enters into force
items.body: A new EU directive on water pollutants took effect this week. Industries and water utilities now face stricter controls on more substances.

items.headline: A political dynasty heiress and a former trade minister advance to Peru's presidential runoff
items.body: Peru's final vote count confirmed a polarized presidential runoff election. Voters and investors now face a stark choice between two ideological extremes.

items.headline: Democratic Republic of the Congo confirms new Ebola outbreak, WHO scales up support
items.body: The Democratic Republic of Congo declared a new Ebola outbreak. An emergency response is now underway affecting residents and health workers.

es.headline: Spain ends tax relief measures for electricity and gas
es.body: The government is ending temporary VAT reductions on electricity and natural gas. The move raises utility costs for households and businesses starting in June.

no.headline: Norway joins international effort to establish special tribunal for Ukraine
no.body: Norway joined 35 other countries to create a new special tribunal. The body will investigate and prosecute Russia's crime of aggression.

uk.headline: UK economy posts unexpected growth in first quarter
uk.body: The United Kingdom's economy grew unexpectedly during the year's first quarter. The data challenges recession forecasts and complicates central bank decisions.

uk.headline: Government bill reverses mandatory sale of council homes
uk.body: A new bill ends the forced sale of high-value social housing. Local authorities now have greater power to manage their housing stock.
